The World Health Organization launched an intensified plan to eradicate smallpox in 1967. Widespread immunization and surveillance were conducted around the world for several years. The last known natural case was in Somalia in 1977. In 1980 WHO declared smallpox eradicated – the only infectious disease to achieve this distinction.
translations.hypothesis: It was observed that those who had contracted bovine tuberculosis did not develop scars, and this suggested that a similar method could be used to prevent scarring in other individuals. The World Health Organization launched a comprehensive program to eradicate smallpox in 1967. Widespread vaccination and surveillance were carried out globally for several years. The last known natural case of smallpox occurred in Somalia in 1977. In 1980, the WHO declared that smallpox had been eradicated – a remarkable achievement in the fight against infectious diseases.

translations.source: Lokhu kuhlala kuphakathi kwempumelelo yezempilo yomphakathi ephawuleka kakhulu futhi ejulile emlandweni. Izimpawu: Izimpawu zokuqala zengxibongo zihlanganisa ukushisa okukhulu, ukukhathala nobuhlungu obukhulu emhlane, futhi kancane kancane, ubuhlungu besisu nokuhlanza. Ezinsukwini ezimbili kuya kwezingu-3 kamuva igciwane likhiqiza ukuqubuka kwesici esinamaqhuqhuva agcwele uketshezi olucacile, kamuva olugcwala ubomvu futhi ekugcineni lube noqweqwe oluyoma luwe. Ukuqubuka kuqala ebusweni nasezandleni, bese kudlulela kuwo wonke umzimba. Izilonda ziyakhula kulwelwesi lwamafinyila ekhaleni nasemlonyeni futhi zibe nesilonda ngokushesha ngemva kokwakheka.
translations.reference: This remains among the most notable and profound public health successes in history. Symptoms: Early symptoms of smallpox include high fever, fatigue and severe back pain, and less often, abdominal pain and vomiting. Two to 3 days later the virus produces a characteristic rash with bumps full of a clear liquid, which later fill with pus and finally develop a crust that dries and falls off. The rash begins on the face and hands, then spreads to the rest of the body. Lesions develop in the mucous membranes of the nose and mouth and ulcerate soon after formation.
translations.hypothesis: This is a significant and well-documented case in the history of public health. Symptoms: The initial symptoms of smallpox include a high fever, malaise, and severe back pain, followed by abdominal pain and vomiting. Within two to three days, the virus produces a rash of small, red spots, which gradually become filled with a clear fluid, then pus, and eventually form a crust that falls off. The rash typically begins on the face and hands, then spreads to the rest of the body. The lesions develop into blisters in the mouth and throat, and quickly become ulcers.

translations.source: Ingxibongo isakazwa isuka kumuntu iye komunye ngamaconsi athelelekayo lapho usondelana nabantu abanalesi sifo abanezimpawu zalesi sifo, noma kwezinye izimo ngezingubo ezingcolile kanye nezingubo zokulala. Inesikhathi sokufukamela esiyizinsuku eziyisi-7-17 ngemuva kokuchayeka futhi ithathelwana kuphela uma umkhuhlane uqala. Abantu bahlala bethelelana kuze kuqhume utwayi lokugcina. Ingxibongo yabulala abantu abangafika ku-30%.Ikhadi lokuqaphela ingxibongo yeWHO Impendulo yeWHO: Isikhathi kusukela ekuqothulweni sichazwe yinkulumompikiswano ende neyinkimbinkimbi egxile ekubhujisweni kwesitoko sokugcina esisele segciwane le-variola eliphilayo.
translations.reference: Smallpox is transmitted from person to person via infective droplets during close contact with infected people who have symptoms of the disease, or in some cases through contaminated clothing and bedding. It has an incubation period of 7–17 days after exposure and only becomes infectious once a fever develops. People remain infectious until the last scabs fall off. Smallpox was fatal in up to 30% of cases.WHO smallpox recognition card WHO response: The period since eradication has been defined by a lengthy and complex debate focussed on the destruction of the last remaining stocks of live variola virus.
translations.hypothesis: Smallpox is transmitted from person to person through direct contact with infected individuals who have symptoms of the disease, or through contact with contaminated clothing or bedding. It has an incubation period of 7-17 days and is contagious only after the rash appears. People remain contagious until all pustules have crusted over. Smallpox was responsible for up to 30% of deaths. WHO Smallpox Fact Sheet: The period from infection is defined by a lengthy and complex process focused on the destruction of any remaining stocks of the live variola virus.

translations.source: Ngo-1996, eMhlanganweni Wezempilo Womhlaba wama-49, Amazwe Angamalungu anquma ukuthi lesi sitoko sicekelwe phansi ngo-June 1999, futhi kuphela ukuvumela amasampula amancane ukuba agcinwe ngenjongo yocwaningo kumalabhorethri amabili aqokiwe avikelekile - eyodwa e-United States kanye neyodwa eRussia. Inhlangano. Ukugcinwa kwesikhashana kwezitokwe ezikhona zegciwane le-variola kusetshenziselwa ucwaningo olwengeziwe olubalulekile, oluqondiswe yiKomidi Lokweluleka le-Variola Virus Research. Leli qembu lihlangana njalo ngonyaka. IHhovisi likaNobhala Wengxibongo yeWHO elizinze ekomkhulu leWHO liqondisa yonke imisebenzi ehlobene nokuqeda ingxibongo kanye nangemuva kokuqeda. Lokhu kufaka phakathi ukwengamela ucwaningo oluvunyelwe ngegciwane le-variola eliphilayo ukuze kuthuthukiswe izindlela eziphikisayo ezifana nomuthi wokugoma nezidambisigciwane.
translations.reference: In 1996, at the 49th World Health Assembly, Member States decided to have this stock destroyed in June 1999, and only to allow small samples to be kept for research purposes at two designated secure laboratories – one in the United States and one in the Russian Federation. Temporary retention of the existing stocks of variola virus are used for further essential research, which is overseen by the Advisory Committee for Variola Virus Research. This group meets on an annual basis. The WHO Smallpox Secretariat based in WHO Headquarters coordinates all smallpox eradication-related and post-eradication activities. These include overseeing permitted research with live variola virus for the development of countermeasures such as vaccine and antivirals.
translations.hypothesis: In 1996, at the 49th World Health Assembly, member states decided that this stockpile should be phased out by June 1999, with only small samples being maintained for research purposes in two secure, high-containment laboratories – one in the United States and one in Russia. The temporary storage of existing variola virus stocks is used for further important research, overseen by the Variola Virus Research Advisory Committee. This committee meets annually. The WHO's Office of the Assistant Director-General for Communicable Diseases directs all activities related to the elimination and post-elimination phases. This includes facilitating research using live variola virus to develop countermeasures such as antiviral drugs and vaccines.

translations.source: Kusukela esikhathini sokuqedwa, imithi yokugoma ephephile kanye nokwelashwa okuqondile kuye kwathuthukiswa ingxibongo nezifo ezihlobene nazo ezifana nenkawu. Ihhovisi likaNobhala nalo linesibopho sokulungela izimo eziphuthumayo futhi lilawula inqwaba yezimo eziphuthumayo zomuthi wokugomela ingxibongo esimweni esingenakwenzeka ukuthi isifo siphinde sivele. Ihhovisi likaNobhala lilawula ukuhlolwa kwebiosafety kanye nebiosecurity kwezindawo zokugcina amagciwane evariola ezigunyaziwe njengoba kugunyazwe uMkhandlu Wezempilo Womhlaba, futhi iqinisekisa ukubika kwaminyaka yonke Emikhandlwini Ebusayo yeWHO. I-Smartpox Secretariat isekela iWHO Archives ekulondolozeni umlando womhlaba wonke wengxibongo kanye nokubhala phansi izifundo ezitholwe ekuqedeni ngempumelelo ingxibongo. Izindima nemisebenzi yamanje yeWHO Smallpox Secretariat ihlanganisa:
translations.reference: Since the eradication era, safer vaccines and specific treatments have been developed for smallpox and related diseases such as monkeypox. The Secretariat is also responsible for emergency preparedness and manages a smallpox vaccine emergency stockpile in the unlikely event that the disease re-emerges. The Secretariat manages the biosafety and biosecurity inspections of the authorized variola virus repositories as mandated by the World Health Assembly, and ensures annual reporting to WHO Governing Bodies. The Smallpox Secretariat supports WHO Archives in preserving the global history of smallpox and documenting the lessons learned from successful eradication of smallpox. The WHO Smallpox Secretariat's current roles and responsibilities include:
translations.hypothesis: Since its eradication, safe vaccines and targeted treatments have been developed for diseases similar to smallpox. The Secretariat also has a responsibility to prepare for potential outbreaks and manages a range of emergency situations related to smallpox vaccination in the unlikely event that the disease should reappear. The Secretariat oversees the biosafety and biosecurity of facilities that store variola virus, as mandated by the World Health Organization, and ensures annual reporting to the WHO's governing bodies. The Smartpox Secretariat supports the WHO Archives in preserving the global history of smallpox and documenting the lessons learned from its successful eradication. The current roles and responsibilities of the WHO Smallpox Secretariat include:

translations.source: Ucwaningo lwegciwane leVariola kanye nokuthuthukiswa kwezinyathelo zokulwa ukuhlela imihlangano yaminyaka yonke yeWHO Advisory Committee on Variola Virus Research (ACVVR) ukuxhumanisa ukubuyekezwa kweziphakamiso zocwaningo zokusebenza nge-variola virus ngeACVVR ukuphatha ubudlelwano nezinqolobane ezimbili zegciwane le-variola (eziphinde ziyizikhungo ezisebenzisana neWHO) ukurekhoda, ukucubungula kanye nokuqapha izicelo ezivela kubacwaningi be-variola virus DNA kusuka kumakhosombe we-2 ngaphansi kwemithetho ethile
translations.reference: Variola virus research and development of countermeasures organizing annual meetings of the WHO Advisory Committee on Variola Virus Research (ACVVR) coordinating review of research proposals for work with variola virus through the ACVVR managing relationships with the two variola virus repositories (which are also WHO Collaborating Centres) recording, processing and monitoring requests from researchers for variola virus DNA from the 2 repositories under specific rules
translations.hypothesis: The research on the Variola virus and the development of countermeasures involve annual meetings of the WHO Advisory Committee on Variola Virus Research (ACVVR) to connect the review of research proposals related to the Variola virus with the ACVVR. This also includes managing relationships with two specialized virus repositories (which also collaborate with the WHO) and recording, processing, and monitoring requests from researchers for Variola virus DNA samples, under specific regulations.

translations.source: ukwenza ukuhlolwa kwe-biosafety kanye ne-biosecurity ezindaweni ezi-2 zomhlaba jikelele zevariola virus ukuthuthukisa isiqondiso esivamile mayelana nocwaningo nge-variola virus DNA kanye nezindaba ezihlobene Ukulungela izimo eziphuthumayo kanye nokusabela ukuxhumanisa nokulungiselela isiqondiso esijwayelekile ekuthuthukisweni nasekusetshenzisweni kwezinyathelo zezokwelapha zama-orthopoxviruses ukuthuthukisa amasu okulungela nokuphendula, kanye namathuluzi okusebenzisa izinyathelo zokulungela
translations.reference: conducting biennial biosafety and biosecurity inspections of the 2 global variola virus repositories developing normative guidance regarding research on variola virus DNA and related matters Emergency preparedness and response coordinating and preparing normative guidance on development and use of medical countermeasures for orthopoxviruses developing preparedness and response strategies, and tools to implement preparedness measures
translations.hypothesis: Conducting biosafety and biosecurity assessments in two global locations for the variola virus to improve general guidance on research involving variola virus DNA and related matters.  Preparing for potential outbreaks and responding effectively by linking preparedness with established guidance on the development and use of countermeasures for orthopoxviruses, to enhance preparedness and response strategies, and to facilitate the implementation of preparedness measures.

translations.source: ukugcina nokuphatha isitokwe somgomo wengxibongo emhlabeni wonke ukuxhumanisa ukuthuthukiswa kwenethiwekhi yaselabhorethri yokuthola i-orthopoxvirus kanye nokuxilongwa ukuthuthukisa isiqondiso esivamile kanye namasu okuphendula inkawu yabantu namanye ama-orthopoxvirus ukusekela ukwakhiwa kwamandla nokuthuthukisa izinto zokuqeqesha ukufaka isandla ezinhlelweni zokuphepha kwezempilo emhlabeni jikelele
translations.reference: maintaining and managing the smallpox global vaccine stockpile coordinating development of a laboratory network for orthopoxvirus detection and diagnostics developing normative guidance and response strategies for human monkeypox and other orthopoxviruses supporting capacity-building and developing training materials contributing to global health security initiatives
translations.hypothesis: To maintain and manage a global network for the detection of smallpox, it is necessary to connect the development of laboratory networks for orthopoxvirus detection with the standardization of diagnostic methods and response strategies for smallpox and other orthopoxviruses. This will support the strengthening of global health security and the improvement of training resources for participation in global health security programs.

translations.source: Ukugcina umlando wengxibongo yomhlaba wonke ukwengamela izinqolobane zoHlelo Lokuqedwa Kwengxibongo ngokubambisana neWHO Archives kanye neWHO Library okuholela ezingxoxweni zenqubomgomo kanye nemicimbi yesikhumbuzo emayelana nokuqedwa kwengxibongo ukubika minyaka yonke kuWHO Executive Board kanye neWorld Health Assembly IHhovisi likaNobhala liphinde lihlanganise futhi lilawule uxhaso lwezimali lwalemsebenzi kanye nemicimbi yesikhashana njengoba kudingeka njengokubonisana kwezobuchwepheshe nokuqeqeshwa.
translations.reference: Preserving global smallpox history overseeing the Smallpox Eradication Programme archives in collaboration with WHO Archives and the WHO Library leading on policy discussions and commemorative events regarding smallpox eradication reporting annually to the WHO Executive Board and the World Health Assembly The Secretariat also mobilizes and manages funding for these activities and for ad hoc events as required such as technical consultations and training.
translations.hypothesis: To preserve the global history of smallpox eradication, the program collaborates with the WHO Archives and the WHO Library to facilitate policy discussions and commemorative events related to smallpox eradication. It also provides annual reports to the WHO Executive Board and the World Health Assembly. The office also coordinates and manages funding for this work, as well as for temporary events, technical consultations, and training activities.

translations.source: ISnakebite envenoming Uhlolojikelele: iSnakebite envenoming yisifo esingase sibe yingozi empilweni esibangelwa ubuthi lapho elunywa inyoka enesihlungu. i-envenoming ingabangelwa futhi ukufuthwa ngobuthi emehlweni yizinhlobo ezithile zezinyoka ezikwazi ukukhafula ubuthi njengendlela yokuzivikela. Imizamo eyanele yesikhathi esidlule yokulawula ukusabalala kwenyoka ikhiqize idatha ehlukene, enganembile ye-epidemiological. Izisulu eziningi aziyi ezikhungweni zezempilo noma ezibhedlela kunalokho zithembele ekwelashweni kwesintu.
translations.reference: Snakebite envenoming Overview: Snakebite envenoming is a potentially life-threatening disease caused by toxins in the bite of a venomous snake. Envenoming can also be caused by having venom sprayed into the eyes by certain species of snakes that have the ability to spit venom as a defence measure. Inadequate past efforts to control snakebite envenoming has produced fragmented, inaccurate epidemiological data. Many victims do not attend health centres or hospitals and instead rely on traditional treatments.
translations.hypothesis: Snakebite envenomation: Snakebite envenomation is a potentially life-threatening condition caused by the injection of venom from a venomous snake. Envenomation can also be caused by the introduction of venom into the eyes by certain types of snakes as a defense mechanism. Past efforts to control snakebite have yielded diverse and often unreliable epidemiological data. Many cases go unreported, with victims often relying on traditional remedies rather than seeking medical attention.

translations.source: Kodwa ke, imininingwane etholakalayo ikhombisa ukuthi abantu abayizigidi ezi-4.5–5.4 balunywa izinyoka minyaka yonke. Kulokhu, bangu-1.8-2.7 miliyoni abanesifo somtholampilo kanti abangu-81 000 kuya ku-138 000 bashona ngenxa yezinkinga. Amaqembu asengozini enkulu ahlanganisa abasebenzi bezolimo basezindaweni zasemakhaya, abelusi, abadobi, abazingeli, izingane ezisebenzayo, abantu abahlala ezindlini ezingakhiwe kahle kanye nalabo abangenayo imfundo nokunakekelwa kwezempilo okulinganiselwe. Ukugula nokufa kwenzeka kaningi phakathi kwentsha futhi izingane zisengozini enkulu yokufa. Ngaphezu kwalokho, abesifazane baba nezithiyo ezengeziwe zokuthola usizo lwezokwelapha kwamanye amasiko futhi abesifazane abakhulelwe basengozini enkulu.
translations.reference: However, available data show 4.5–5.4 million people get bitten by snakes annually. Of this, 1.8–2.7 million develop clinical illness and 81 000 to 138 000 die from complications. High-risk groups include rural agricultural workers, herders, fishermen, hunters, working children, people living in poorly constructed houses and those with limited access to education and healthcare. Morbidity and mortality occur most frequently among young people and children suffer higher case fatality. Furthermore, women experience increased barriers to accessing medical care in some cultures and pregnant women are extremely vulnerable.
translations.hypothesis: However, available data indicates that approximately 4.5 to 5.4 million people are bitten by snakes each year. Of these, 1.8 to 2.7 million people develop clinical illness, and between 81,000 and 138,000 people die as a result of the envenomation. The most vulnerable groups include agricultural workers in rural areas, herders, construction workers, fishermen, child laborers, people living in poorly constructed dwellings, and those with limited access to education and healthcare. Illness and death are most common among young people, and children are at a particularly high risk of mortality. Furthermore, women in some cultures face additional barriers to accessing medical care, and pregnant women are at increased risk.

translations.source: Inkinga eqhubekayo ekhawulela ukufinyelela ekwelashweni okuphephile, okusebenzayo kwe-antivenin ezifundeni eziningi, futhi ikakhulukazi iSub-Saharan Africa, ingenye yezinto ezinomthelela ekwakhekeni kokufuna usizo ngemithi yendabuko. Izimpawu: Ukulunywa noma isihlungu esifuthwe ezinyokaneni ezinobuthi kungabangela uchungechunge lwezimo eziphuthumayo ezibucayi nezibucayi. Ukutheleleka ezinhlotsheni ezihlukene zezinyoka kungabangela izimpawu ezihlukene, ezinye ezimbi kakhulu kunezinye. Lokhu kwenza ukulungiswa kwama-antivenin alungile kube inkinga eqhubekayo. I-envenoming ingabangela ukukhubazeka okukhulu okungase kuvimbele ukuphefumula, okwenza ukunakekelwa kwezokwelapha ngokushesha kube bucayi.
translations.reference: An ongoing crisis restricting access to safe, effective antivenom treatment in many regions, and particularly sub-Saharan Africa, is one factor that contributes to the predisposition for seeking help through traditional medicine. Symptoms: Bites or sprayed venom from venomous snakes can cause a range of acute and serious medical emergencies. Envenoming from different types of snakes can cause different symptoms, some more serious than others. This makes the preparation of correct antivenoms an ongoing problem. Envenoming can cause severe paralysis that may prevent breathing, making immediate medical attention critical.
translations.hypothesis: The ongoing challenge of limited access to safe and effective antivenom in many regions, particularly in Sub-Saharan Africa, is a significant factor influencing the use of traditional medicine. Symptoms: Exposure to venom from various snake species can cause a range of acute and potentially life-threatening conditions. Different types of snake venom can produce different symptoms, with some being more severe than others. This makes the appropriate selection of antivenom a persistent problem. Envenomation can lead to serious complications that may compromise breathing, making immediate medical care critical.

translations.source: Abantu bangase futhi babe nenkinga yokopha okungaholela ekopheni okubulalayo noma ukwehluleka kwezinso okungenakulungiseka. Ukucekelwa phansi okukhulu kwezicubu zendawo nakho kungase kwenzeke, okungaholela ekukhubazekeni unomphela ngisho nokunqunywa kwesitho. Izingane zisengozini enkulu yemiphumela emibi ngenxa yesisindo somzimba esiphansi kunabantu abadala. Ukufa okuningi kanye nemiphumela emibi ngenxa yokulunywa yizinyoka kungavinjelwa ngokuphelele ngokwenza ama-antivenin aphephile futhi asebenzayo atholakale kabanzi futhi afinyeleleke, ikakhulukazi ezindaweni ezisengozini enkulu. Izinqamuleli zezinyoka zekhwalithi ephezulu ukuphela kwendlela yokwelapha esebenzayo yokuvimbela noma ukubuyisela emuva iningi lemiphumela enobuthi yokulunywa yizinyoka.
translations.reference: People may also experience bleeding disorders that can lead to fatal haemorrhages or irreversible kidney failure. Severe local tissue destruction may also occur, which can lead to permanent disability and even limb amputation. Children are at higher risk of severe effects due to lower body mass than adults. Most deaths and serious consequences from snake bites are entirely preventable by making safe and effective antivenoms more widely available and accessible, particularly in high-risk areas. High quality snake antivenoms are the only effective treatment to prevent or reverse most of the venomous effects of snake bites.
translations.hypothesis: People may also experience complications from bites, which can lead to fatal infections or kidney failure that is not reversible. Extensive tissue damage can also occur, potentially resulting in permanent disability or even amputation. Children are at a higher risk of severe complications due to their lower body weight. Many deaths and serious complications resulting from snakebites could be prevented if safe and effective antivenoms were more widely available and accessible, particularly in high-risk areas. High-quality snake antivenoms are a crucial part of an effective strategy for preventing or reversing the harmful effects of snakebites.

translations.source: Ukwelashwa: Ama-antivenin enyoka ayizindlela zokwelapha ezisebenzayo zokuvimbela noma ukubuyisela emuva iningi lemiphumela eyingozi yokulunywa yinyoka futhi afakwe ohlwini lweWHO lwemithi ebalulekile. Ukutholakala kanye nokufinyeleleka kwalawa ma-antivenin, kanye nokuqwashisa ngezindlela eziyinhloko zokuvimbela phakathi kwemiphakathi kanye nabasebenzi bezempilo, izindlela ezingcono kakhulu zokukhawulela imiphumela emibi kanye nokufa okubangelwa yizinyoka. Ngemva kokulunywa yinyoka okusolakala ukuthi inobuthi, landela lezi zinyathelo: Suka ngokushesha endaweni lapho ukuluma kwenzeke khona. Susa noma yini eqinile endaweni elunywayo ukuze ugweme ukulimala uma kwenzeka ukuvuvukala.
translations.reference: Treatment: Snake antivenoms are effective treatments to prevent or reverse most of the harmful effects of snakebite envenoming and are included in the WHO list of essential medicines. The availability and accessibility of these antivenoms, along with raising awareness on primary prevention methods among communities and health workers, are the best ways to limit serious consequences and deaths from snakebite envenoming. After a bite by a snake suspected of being venomous, follow these steps: Immediately move away from the area where the bite occurred. Remove anything tight from around the bitten part of the body to avoid harm if swelling occurs.
translations.hypothesis: Treatment: Antivenom is an effective treatment for preventing or reversing many of the dangerous effects of snake bites, and it is included in the WHO's list of essential medicines. The availability and accessibility of these antivenoms, along with public awareness and education about snake bite prevention among communities and healthcare workers, are the best ways to limit the negative consequences and fatalities caused by snakes. If someone is bitten by a snake and you suspect it is venomous, follow these steps: Immediately move away from the area where the bite occurred. Remove any constricting items from the affected limb to prevent further damage in case of swelling.

translations.source: Qinisekisa isisulu, njengoba ukulunywa izinyoka eziningi ezinobuthi akubangeli ukufa ngokushesha. Misa umuntu ngokuphelele futhi umhambise esikhungweni sezempilo ngokushesha okukhulu Ukufaka ingcindezi endaweni yokuluma ngephedi yokucindezela kungase kulungele kwezinye izimo. Gwema izindlela zosizo lokuqala noma imithi yesintu. IParacetamol ingase inikezwe ubuhlungu bendawo (okungaba nzima kakhulu).
translations.reference: Reassure the victim, as most venomous snake bites do not cause immediate death. Immobilize the person completely and transport the person to a health facility as soon as possible Applying pressure at the bite site with a pressure pad may be suitable in some cases. Avoid traditional first aid methods or herbal medicines. Paracetamol may be given for local pain (which can be severe).
translations.hypothesis: Confirm the species of snake, as many venomous snake bites do not cause immediate death. Stop the person from moving and transport them to a medical facility as quickly as possible. Applying pressure to the bite site with a pressure bandage may be helpful in some situations. Avoid any traditional remedies or first aid techniques that could worsen the situation. Paracetamol (acetaminophen) may be given for local pain (which can be severe).

translations.source: Ukuhlanza kungase kwenzeke, ngakho ke beka umuntu ohlangothini lwakhe lwesobunxele endaweni yokululama. Gada eduze kwendlela yokuphefumula nokuphefumula futhi ulungele ukuvuselela uma kunesidingo
translations.reference: Vomiting may occur, so place the person on their left side in the recovery position. Closely monitor airway and breathing and be ready to resuscitate if necessary
translations.hypothesis: Cleaning may be necessary, so position someone on their left side in the recovery position. Monitor their breathing and be prepared to administer rescue breaths if needed.

translations.source: IHelminthiases edluliselwa emhlabathini Uhlolojikelele: Ukutheleleka kweSoil-transmitted helminth (STH) kuphakathi kwezifo ezivame kakhulu emhlabeni wonke futhi kuthinta imiphakathi empofu kakhulu nentula kakhulu. Atheleleka ngamaqanda akhona endle yabantu abuye angcolise umhlabathi ezindaweni lapho ukuthuthwa kwendle kungalungile. Izinhlobo eziyinhloko ezithelela abantu izikelemu eziyindilinga (Ascaris lumbricoides), umswane (Trichuris trichiura) kanye nezikelemu (Necator americanus kanye ne-Ancylostoma duodenale). Izikelemu zamathumbu ziveza izimpawu eziningi ezihlanganisa ukuvela kwamathumbu (uhudo, ubuhlungu besisu), ukugula okujwayelekile kanye nokuba buthakathaka.
translations.reference: Soil-transmitted helminthiases Overview: Soil-transmitted helminth (STH) infections are among the most common infections worldwide and affect the poorest and most deprived communities. They are transmitted by eggs present in human faeces which in turn contaminate soil in areas where sanitation is poor. The main species that infect people are the roundworm (Ascaris lumbricoides), the whipworm (Trichuris trichiura) and the hookworms (Necator americanus and Ancylostoma duodenale). Intestinal worms produce a wide range of symptoms including intestinal manifestations (diarrhoea, abdominal pain), general malaise and weakness.
translations.hypothesis: Soil-transmitted helminthiases: Overview: Soil-transmitted helminth (STH) infections are among the most common parasitic diseases worldwide and disproportionately affect the poorest and most vulnerable populations. These infections are caused by eggs present in the environment that contaminate the soil, particularly in areas with poor sanitation. The main species that infect humans are roundworms (Ascaris lumbricoides), whipworms (Trichuris trichiura), and hookworms (Necator americanus and Ancylostoma duodenale). Intestinal worms can cause a variety of symptoms, including diarrhea, abdominal pain, anemia, and malnutrition.

translations.source: Izikelemu zehookworms zidala ukulahleka kwegazi okungapheli emathunjini okuholela ku-anemia. Amahelminths adluliselwa emhlabathini asakazwa ngamaqanda adluliselwa endle yabantu abanaleli gciwane. Izikelemu zabantu abadala zihlala emathunjini lapho zikhiqiza izinkulungwane zamaqanda usuku ngalunye. Ezindaweni ezingenazo izindlu zokukhucululwa kwendle ezanele, la maqanda angcolisa umhlabathi. Lokhu kungenzeka ngezindlela eziningi: amaqanda ahlanganiswe nemifino adliwa lapho imifino ingaphekwa ngokucophelela, igezwe noma ingahlutshiwe;
translations.reference: Hookworms cause chronic intestinal blood loss that result in anaemia. Soil-transmitted helminths are transmitted by eggs that are passed in the faeces of infected people. Adult worms live in the intestine where they produce thousands of eggs each day. In areas that lack adequate sanitation, these eggs contaminate the soil. This can happen in several ways: eggs that are attached to vegetables are ingested when the vegetables are not carefully cooked, washed or peeled;
translations.hypothesis: Hookworm infections cause chronic blood loss in the intestines, leading to anemia. These parasitic worms, which live in the soil, are transmitted to humans through eggs that are shed into the environment by infected individuals. Adult worms reside in the intestines, where they produce thousands of eggs each day. In areas with inadequate sanitation, these eggs can contaminate the soil. This can happen in several ways: eggs can be mixed with manure used as fertilizer, and if the vegetables grown in that soil are not thoroughly washed or cooked, they can be ingested;

translations.source: amaqanda adliwa emithonjeni yamanzi angcolile; amaqanda adliwa yizingane ezidlala emhlabathini onamagciwane bese zifaka izandla emlonyeni zingawagezi. Izimpawu: Ukugula kuhlobene nenani lezikelemu ezihlala echwebeni. Abantu abanezifo zokuqina kokukhanya (izikelemu ezimbalwa) ngokuvamile abaphathwa yilesi sifo. Ukutheleleka okunzima kakhulu kungabangela izinhlobonhlobo zezimpawu ezihlanganisa ukuvela kwamathumbu (uhudo nobuhlungu besisu), ukungondleki, ukugula okujwayelekile kanye nokuba buthakathaka, kanye nokukhubazeka kokukhula nokukhula komzimba.
translations.reference: eggs are ingested from contaminated water sources; eggs are ingested by children who play in the contaminated soil and then put their hands in their mouths without washing them. Symptoms: Morbidity is related to the number of worms harboured. People with infections of light intensity (few worms) usually do not suffer from the infection. Heavier infections can cause a range of symptoms including intestinal manifestations (diarrhoea and abdominal pain), malnutrition, general malaise and weakness, and impaired growth and physical development.
translations.hypothesis: Eggs are consumed from contaminated water sources; children playing in contaminated soil may ingest eggs and then touch their mouths without washing their hands. Symptoms: The severity of the illness is related to the number of cysts ingested. People with weakened immune systems (a small number of cysts) are typically affected. Severe infections can cause a variety of symptoms, including gastrointestinal problems (diarrhea and abdominal pain), nausea, general malaise, and fatigue, as well as stunted growth and development.

translations.source: Amagciwane anamandla aphezulu kakhulu angabangela ukuvinjelwa kwamathumbu okufanele aphathwe ngokuhlinzwa. I-S. stercoralis ingase ibangele izifo zesikhumba kanye negastro-intestinal futhi yaziwa nangokuthi ihlotshaniswa nokungondleki okungapheli ezinganeni. Esimeni sokuncipha kokuzivikela komzimba, iparasite ingabangela ihyperinfection/dissemination syndrome ehlala ibulala uma ingelapheki ngokushesha nangendlela efanele futhi ngokuvamile ebulalayo naphezu kokwelashwa. Ngo-2001, izihambeli zoMhlangano Wezempilo Womhlaba wonke zavumelana ngazwi linye isinqumo (WHA54.19) esinxusa amazwe anesifo esiwumqedazwe ukuthi aqale ukulwa nezikelemu, ikakhulukazi isifo sohudo kanye ne-helminths edluliselwa emhlabathini. Isu lokulawula izifo ze-helminth ezidluliselwa emhlabathini ukulawula izifo ngokwelashwa ngezikhathi ezithile kwabantu abasengozini abahlala ezindaweni ezihlala njalo.
translations.reference: Infections of very high intensity can cause intestinal obstruction that should be treated surgically. S. stercoralis may cause dermatological and gastro-intestinal morbidity and is also known to be associated with chronic malnutrition in children. In case of reduced host immunity, the parasite can cause the hyperinfection/dissemination syndrome that is invariably fatal if not promptly and properly cured and is often fatal despite the treatment. In 2001, delegates at the World Health Assembly unanimously endorsed a resolution (WHA54.19) urging endemic countries to start seriously tackling worms, specifically schistosomiasis and soil-transmitted helminths. The strategy for control of soil-transmitted helminth infections is to control morbidity through the periodic treatment of at-risk people living in endemic areas.
translations.hypothesis: Certain types of intestinal parasites require surgical intervention for proper treatment. *S. stercoralis* can cause skin lesions and gastrointestinal problems, and is also known to be associated with chronic malnutrition in children. In individuals with weakened immune systems, this parasite can cause a hyperinfection/dissemination syndrome, which is often fatal if not treated promptly and appropriately, and can be fatal even with treatment. In 2001, delegates at the World Health Assembly unanimously agreed on a resolution (WHA54.19) urging countries with a high burden of disease to prioritize the control of neglected tropical diseases, particularly intestinal worms and soil-transmitted helminths. A key strategy for controlling soil-transmitted helminth infections is to control the disease through periodic treatment of at-risk populations living in endemic areas.

translations.source: Abantu abasengozini yilaba: izingane zasenkulisa izingane zesikole abesifazane abaneminyaka yobudala yokuzala (kuhlanganise nabesifazane abakhulelwe ku-trimester yesibili neyesithathu kanye nabesifazane abancelisayo) abantu abadala emisebenzini ethile engcuphe kakhulu njengabacoshi betiye noma abavukuzi.
translations.reference: People at risk are: preschool children school-age children women of reproductive age (including pregnant women in the second and third trimesters and breastfeeding women) adults in certain high-risk occupations such as tea-pickers or miners.
translations.hypothesis: The groups at risk include: pregnant women, school children, women of childbearing age (including those in their second and third trimesters of pregnancy and women who are trying to conceive), and adults working in particularly hazardous occupations such as tea pickers or miners.

translations.source: UkwelashwaImithi etuswa yiWHO – i-albendazole (400 mg) kanye ne-mebendazole (500 mg) – iyasebenza, ayibizi futhi kulula ukuyinikezwa yizisebenzi ezingezona ezokwelashwa (isb. othisha). Adlule ekuhlolweni okubanzi kokuphepha futhi asetshenziswe ezigidini zabantu abanemiphumela engemihle embalwa noma emincane. Kokubili i-albendazole ne-mebendazole inikelwa ezindlini zezempilo zikazwelonke ngeWHO kuwo wonke amazwe anesifo ukuze zelashwe zonke izingane ezisafunda. I-Ivermectin yokulawula kwe-S. stercoralis kulindeleke ukuthi itholakale ngentengo ethengekayo kusukela ngo-2021.
translations.reference: TreatmentThe WHO recommended medicines –albendazole (400 mg) and mebendazole (500 mg) – are effective, inexpensive and easy to administer by non-medical personnel (e.g. teachers). They have been through extensive safety testing and have been used in millions of people with few and minor side-effects. Both albendazole and mebendazole are donated to national ministries of health through WHO in all endemic countries for the treatment of all children of school age. Ivermectin for the control of S. stercoralis is expected to be available at affordable price from 2021.
translations.hypothesis: Treatment: The medications recommended by the WHO – albendazole (400 mg) and mebendazole (500 mg) – are effective, affordable, and easy to administer by non-medical personnel (e.g., teachers). They have undergone extensive safety testing and have been used in large populations with few or minor side effects. Both albendazole and mebendazole are provided by the WHO to health centers in all countries affected by the disease, to ensure that all school-aged children can be treated. Ivermectin, for the control of *S. stercoralis*, is expected to become available at an affordable price starting in 2021.

translations.source: Ukubeletha umtwana oseshonile Uhlolojikelele: Ingane eshona ngemva kwamasonto angu-28 ekhulelwe, kodwa ngaphambi noma ngesikhathi sokuzalwa, ibhekwa njengokuzalwa ishonile. Cishe kunabantwana abazalwa beshonile ababalelwa ezigidini ezi-2 minyaka yonke - olulodwa njalo ngemizuzwana eyi-16. Ngaphezu kwama-40% akho konke ukubeletha kwenzeka ngesikhathi sokubeletha - ukulahlekelwa okungagwenywa ngekhwalithi ethuthukisiwe nokunakekelwa okuhloniphekile ngesikhathi sokubeletha okuhlanganisa ukuqapha okujwayelekile kanye nokufinyelela ngesikhathi ekunakekelweni okuphuthumayo uma kudingeka. Ukuthola umntwana oshonile ngesikhathi sokukhulelwa noma ebeletha kuyinhlekelele engasingathwanga ngokwanele kuma-ajenda omhlaba, izinqubomgomo nezinhlelo ezixhaswa ngemali. Kunezindleko ezingokwengqondo, ikakhulukazi kwabesifazane nemindeni yabo, njengokudangala komama, imiphumela yezezimali kanye nemiphumela yezomnotho, kanye nokucwaswa kanye nokungamukeleki. okuhlosiwe emhlabeni wonke kwabantwana abazalwa beshonile abayi-12 noma ngaphansi kwekota yesithathu (sekwephuzile) kubantwana abayi-1,000 sebephelele kuwo wonke amazwe ngo-2030.
translations.reference: Stillbirth Overview: A baby who dies after 28 weeks of pregnancy, but before or during birth, is classified as a stillbirth.There are nearly 2 million stillbirths every year – one every 16 seconds. Over 40% of all stillbirths occur during labour – a loss that could be avoided with improved quality and respectful care during childbirth including routine monitoring and timely access to emergency obstetric care when required. Experiencing a stillbirth during pregnancy or childbirth is a tragedy insufficiently addressed in global agendas, policies and funded programmes. There are psychological costs, especially to women and their families, such as maternal depression, financial consequences and economic repercussions, as well as stigma and taboo.In 2014, the World Health Assembly endorsed the Every Newborn Action Plan (ENAP), which includes a global target of 12 or fewer third trimester (late) stillbirths per 1,000 total births in every country by 2030.
translations.hypothesis: Stillbirth: Definition: A stillbirth is defined as the death of a fetus after 28 weeks of gestation, but before or during birth. Approximately 2 million babies are stillborn each year – one every 16 seconds. Over 40% of all births result in a stillbirth – a loss that cannot be prevented with improved quality of care and respectful maternity care, including routine monitoring and timely access to emergency care when needed. Addressing stillbirths during pregnancy and childbirth is a global health issue that is not adequately addressed in global agendas, policies, and funded programs. There are significant emotional, financial, and social consequences, particularly for women and their families, including grief, financial burdens, and social stigma. The global target is to reduce the number of stillbirths to less than 12 per 1,000 total births in all countries by 2030.

translations.source: Ngo-2021, amazwe angu-139 ikakhulukazi anemali engenayo ephezulu kanye naphezulu anemali engenayo emaphakathi ayefinyelele lokhu kuhlosiwe, kodwa amazwe angu-56 ngeke ngalinye libe nethagethi ye-ENAP ngo-2030 uma ingenziwa eminye imizamo. Uma izimo zamanje ziqhubeka, izingane eziyizigidi ezingu-15.9 zizozalwa zishonile; cishe isigamu salokhu (izigidi ezingu-7.7, 48%) zizokwenzeka eSub-Saharan Africa. Izimbangela: Ngokunakekelwa kwezempilo okuseqophelweni lonke ngesikhathi sokukhulelwa kanye nokubeletha, ukubeletha okuningi kuyagwemeka. Izimbangela eziyinhloko zokubeletha ingane eshonile zihlanganisa izinkinga eziphathelene nokukhulelwa nokubeletha, ukukhulelwa isikhathi eside, izifo zikamama ezinjengomalaleveva, ugcunsula kanye ne-HIV, izimo zomama ikakhulukazi umfutho wegazi ophakeme, nesifo sikashukela, kanye nokuvinjelwa ukukhula kombungu (lapho usana olungakazalwa lungakwazi ukufinyelela amandla ayo okukhula ngakho ke kuncane kunalokho obekufanele kube yikona). Ukungajwayelekile kokuzalwa kuyimbangela engaphansi kuka-10% yezingane ezizalwa zishonile ezibikwe kuzwelonke emazweni anemali engenayo ephezulu futhi ingxenye yangempela yezilungiselelo zabantu abahola kancane ayaziwa ngenxa yemikhawulo ekuxilongweni.
translations.reference: In 2021, 139 mainly high-income and upper middle-income countries had met this target, but 56 countries will not each the ENAP target by 2030 if further efforts are not made. If current trends continue, 15.9 million babies will be stillborn; nearly half of these (7.7 million, 48%) will occur in sub-Saharan Africa. Causes: With quality health care throughout pregnancy and childbirth, most stillbirths are preventable. The major causes of stillbirth include pregnancy and childbirth-related complications, prolonged pregnancy, maternal infections such as malaria, syphilis and HIV, maternal conditions especially hypertension, and diabetes, and fetal growth restriction (when an unborn baby is unable to achieve its growth potential and therefore smaller than it should be). Congenital abnormalities are responsible for less than 10% of stillbirths reported nationally in high-income countries and the exact proportion in low-income settings is unknown due to limitations in diagnostics.
translations.hypothesis: In 2021, 139 countries, particularly those with high and upper-middle income levels, achieved this target, but 56 countries will not meet the ENAP target by 2030 unless further efforts are made. If current trends continue, 15.9 million babies will be born dead; approximately half of these (7.7 million, or 48%) will occur in Sub-Saharan Africa. Causes: Comprehensive care during pregnancy and childbirth, including multiple births, is crucial. The main causes of stillbirth include problems related to pregnancy and childbirth, prolonged pregnancies, maternal illnesses such as pre-eclampsia, gestational diabetes, and HIV, maternal conditions such as high blood pressure and diabetes, and fetal growth restriction (where the baby is too small for its gestational age). Abnormal births account for less than 10% of all stillbirths reported globally in high-income countries, and the true extent of this factor in low-resource settings is largely unknown due to limitations in data collection.

translations.source: Izinto ezihlobene nobudala bukamama nokuzijwayeza ukubhema nakho kungandisa ingozi yesifo sikamama kanye nokubeletha. Ukubeletha kungavinjelwa ngokuhlela umndeni ukuze kugwenywe ukukhulelwa okungafunwa, impilo enhle kanye nokudla okunomsoco ngaphambi nangesikhathi sokukhulelwa, ukunakekelwa kwekhwalithi nenhlonipho ngesikhathi sokubeletha kanye nokubeletha okuhlanganisa nabasebenzi bezempilo abaqeqeshiwe abafanelekayo kuhlanganise nababelethisi. Ukwelashwa ngogcunsula ekukhulelweni kungase futhi kuvimbele ukubeletha okulinganiselwa ku-200 000, kuyilapho ukuqapha izinga lokushaya kwenhliziyo yengane kanye nokugadwa komsebenzi kanye nokungenelela ngokushesha lapho kudingeka kubalulekile ekuvimbeleni ukubeletha okungama-832 000 phakathi kokubeletha kanye nokunciphisa ukufa kwezingane ezisanda kuzalwa. Ukubeletha kuxhumene kakhulu nezinqumo ezingezinhle zezenhlalo nezomnotho zezempilo. Ukuvimbela nokunakekelwa okusabelayo kufanele kuhlanganiswe ekunakekelweni kwezempilo komama, nangale kwalokho.
translations.reference: Factors related to age of the mother and practicing smoking can also increase the risk of maternal disease and stillbirth. Stillbirths can be prevented through family planning to avoid unwanted pregnancies, good health and nutrition prior to and during pregnancy, quality and respectful antenatal and childbirth care including adequate skilled health personnel including midwives. Syphilis treatment in pregnancy could also prevent an estimated 200 000 stillbirths, while fetal heart rate monitoring and labour surveillance and prompt interventions when needed are crucial for preventing 832 000 intrapartum stillbirths and reducing neonatal deaths. Stillbirths are strongly linked to adverse social and economic determinants of health. Prevention and responsive care need to be integrated across the continuum of maternal health care, and beyond.
translations.hypothesis: Factors related to maternal age and smoking habits can also increase the risk of maternal and infant health complications. Unplanned pregnancies can be prevented through family planning to avoid unwanted pregnancies, promoting healthy lifestyles and proper nutrition before and during pregnancy, providing quality and respectful care during childbirth, and ensuring access to skilled healthcare professionals, including midwives, during delivery. Treatment for infertility can also prevent an estimated 200,000 pregnancies, while monitoring the baby's heart rate and fetal well-being, along with prompt intervention when necessary, is crucial in preventing approximately 832,000 stillbirths and reducing neonatal deaths. Maternal health is closely linked to broader social and economic determinants of health. Therefore, prevention and care should be integrated into comprehensive maternal healthcare.

translations.source: Lokhu kuhlanganisa ukunakekelwa ngenhlonipho nokusekela uma kwenzeka ushona. Ukulalela okwenzekayo kanye namazwi abantu besifazane nemiphakathi yabo kubalulekile ukusiza ukubhekana nezinkinga zokucwaswa okuhambisana nokuzala umntwana oshonile. Impendulo yeWHO: Kuningi okudingeka kwenziwe ukuze kuhlanganiswe ukuvimbela ukubeletha phakathi kwe-ajenda yomhlaba wonke neyezwe yokunakekelwa kwezempilo okusezingeni eliphezulu kwabesifazane, intsha, kanye nezingane. Ngesu Lomhlaba Wonke Lezempilo Zabesifazane, Zezingane kanye Nentsha 2016–2030 nokusebenzisa izincomo ze-All Newborn Action Plan (2014), iWHO isebenzela ukuqeda ukubeletha okungavinjelwa, njengengxenye yemizamo yokuthuthukisa impilo kamama, usana, ingane kanye nentsha. Iningi lalabo abazalwa beshonile abazitholi izincwadi zokuzalwa futhi ababhalisiwe.
translations.reference: This includes respectful and supportive care in the event of a death. Listening to the experiences and voices of women and their communities is essential to help address issues of stigma associated with stillbirth. WHO response: More needs to be done to integrate stillbirth prevention within global and national agendas for high quality health care for women, adolescents, and babies. Through the Global Strategy for Women's, Children's and Adolescents Health 2016–2030 and implementing the recommendations of the Every Newborn Action Plan (2014), WHO is working to end preventable stillbirths, as part of efforts to improve maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health. Most stillbirths do not receive a birth certificate and are not registered.
translations.hypothesis: This includes providing respectful care and support when a woman experiences the loss of a child. Listening to the experiences of women and their communities is crucial to helping them cope with the challenges of pregnancy loss and the death of a child. The WHO's response: Much more needs to be done to integrate the prevention of stillbirths into the global and national agendas for high-quality healthcare for women, adolescents, and children. Through the Global Strategy for Women's, Children's and Adolescents' Health 2016–2030 and by implementing the recommendations of the All Newborn Action Plan (2014), the WHO is working to eliminate preventable stillbirths, as part of efforts to improve the health of mothers, newborns, children, and adolescents. Many of the babies who are stillborn are not registered at birth.

translations.source: Ukwenza ngcono izinhlelo zokubika ukuzalwa kanye nokufa kwezingane ezisanda kuzalwa kuyindaba yamalungelo abantu kanye nemfuneko yokunciphisa ukubeletha. Ukwengeza, kunesidingo sokuqinisa izinhlelo zolwazi lwezempilo olujwayelekile ukuze kuthathwe ngokunembile futhi kubikwe izingane ezizalwa zishonile ukuze kuqashwe futhi kwenziwe ngcono izinga lokunakekelwa. IWHO ihlinzeka ngamathuluzi okusiza amazwe ukuthi athuthukise idatha yawo kukho kokubili ukubeletha nokufa kwezingane ezisanda kuzalwa. IWHO futhi inesiqondiso sokusiza amazwe ukuthi abuyekeze futhi aphenye ukufa komuntu ngamunye ukuze akwazi ukuncoma futhi asebenzise izixazululo zokuvimbela okufanayo esikhathini esizayo kanye nohlelo olujwayelekile lokuhlukanisa ukufa kokubeletha (ICD-PM). IWHO kanye ne-UNICEF bayaseseka ikhwelo lokuthi kuthathwe izinyathelo ezihlangene zokuqeda ukubeletha okungavinjelwa ngokuthi:
translations.reference: Improving systems for reporting births and neonatal deaths is a matter of human rights and a prerequisite for reducing stillbirths. In addition, there is a need to strengthen routine health information systems to accurately capture and report stillbirths for monitoring and improving the quality of care. WHO provides tools to help countries improve their data on both stillbirths and neonatal deaths. WHO also has guidance to help countries review and investigate individual deaths so they can recommend and implement solutions to prevent similar ones in the future and a standard classification system for perinatal deaths (ICD-PM). WHO and UNICEF are supporting a call for collective action to end preventable stillbirths by:
translations.hypothesis: Improving systems for reporting on births and deaths of newborns is a matter of human rights and a necessary step in reducing infant mortality. Furthermore, there is a need to strengthen routine health information systems to accurately record and report on stillbirths, in order to improve the quality of care. The WHO provides tools to help countries improve their data on both births and newborn deaths. The WHO also provides guidance to help countries review and investigate individual cases of stillbirth, so that they can identify and implement effective prevention strategies, and to use the standard classification of causes of death in pregnancy, childbirth and the newborn period (ICD-PM). The WHO and UNICEF are working together to promote coordinated action to eliminate preventable stillbirths.

translations.source: ukwandisa ukuqwashisa nokunciphisa ukucwaswa ukweseka abesifazane nemindeni eshonelwe ukuqinisa izinhlelo zezempilo ukuze zinikeze ukunakekelwa kwekhwalithi ephezulu ukwenza kube kuzwelonke kanye nokwenza izindawo okuhloswe ngazo ukubeletha umtwana oseshonile ukwenza ngcono ukukalwa kwezingane ezizalwa zishonile ukuze kuthuthukiswe ubufakazi nolwazi.
translations.reference: increasing awareness and reducing stigma supporting bereaved women and families strengthening health systems to provide high-quality care nationalizing and localizing stillbirth targets improving measurement of stillbirths to enhance evidence and knowledge.
translations.hypothesis:
To increase awareness and reduce maternal mortality, we will:

*   Support women and families.
*   Strengthen healthcare systems to provide high-quality care.
*   Expand access to and improve the quality of facilities for delivering babies.
*   Improve the recording and analysis of stillbirths to improve data and knowledge.

translations.source: Imikhiqizo yezokwelapha esezingeni eliphansi futhi engamanga Uhlolojikelele: Abantu abangafika ezigidini eziyizinkulungwane ezimbili emhlabeni jikelele abanakho ukufinyelela emithini edingekayo, imigomo, izinsiza zezokwelapha ezihlanganisa ukuxilongwa kwe-in vitro, neminye imikhiqizo yezempilo, okudala isikhala esivamise ukugcwaliswa ngemikhiqizo esezingeni eliphansi nengamanga. Le nkinga iyakhula njengoba amaketanga okuhlinzeka emhlabeni wonke eba yinkimbinkimbi, okusho ukuthi imikhiqizo ekhiqizwa ezweni elilodwa ingase ihlanganiswe ezweni lesibili futhi isatshalaliswe emingceleni ukuze idayiswe noma ithengiswe kubathengi ngengxenye yesithathu. Ukukhula kwe-e-commerce nakho kunomthelela kulo mkhuba ngokwenza kube lula ukuthenga imithi ku-inthanethi, ngokuvamile ivela emithonjeni engagunyaziwe. IWHO ihlonze lolu daba njengenye yezinselelo eziphuthumayo zezempilo kule minyaka eyishumi ezayo uma kubhekwa ukuthi imithi engaphezu kowodwa kweyishumi emazweni anemali engenayo ephansi noma ephakathi ilinganiselwa ukuthi isezingeni eliphansi noma engamanga.
translations.reference: Substandard and falsified medical products Overview: Up to two billion people around the world lack access to necessary medicines, vaccines, medical devices including in vitro diagnostics, and other health products, which creates a vacuum that is too often filled by substandard and falsified products. This problem is growing as global supply chains become more complex, meaning products manufactured in one country may be packaged in a second country and distributed across borders to be marketed or sold to consumers in a third. The growth of e-commerce also contributes to this trend by making it easier to purchase medicines online, often from unauthorized sources. WHO has identified this issue one of the urgent health challenges for the next decade given that more than one in ten medicines in low- and middle-income countries are estimated to be substandard or falsified.
translations.hypothesis: Substandard and counterfeit medical products: Overview: An estimated two billion people worldwide lack access to essential medicines, vaccines, diagnostic tools, and other healthcare products, creating a market that is often exploited by substandard and counterfeit products. This problem is exacerbated by increasingly complex global supply chains, where products manufactured in one country may be mixed with products from another and distributed through various channels to reach consumers, often at a fraction of the original cost. The growth of e-commerce has also contributed to this trend, making it easier to purchase medications online, often from unregulated sources. The WHO has identified this issue as one of the most pressing public health challenges of the next decade, noting that more than one in ten medical products in low- and middle-income countries are estimated to be substandard or counterfeit.

translations.source: Alikho izwe elihlala lingakathintwa kulolu daba, futhi iWHO ithole imibiko yemithi yezokwelapha esezingeni eliphansi noma engamanga, imigomo kanye nokuxilongwa kwe-in vitro okuvela kuzo zonke izifunda zomhlaba. Yomibili imithi ejwayelekile kanye nentsha ingenziwa amanga, kusukela emikhiqizweni ebiza kakhulu yomdlavuza kuye emikhiqizweni engabizi kakhulu yokwelapha izinhlungu. Amazwe Angamalungu eWHO avumelene ngesu eliphelele lomhlaba wonke eligxile ekuvimbeleni, ekuhlonzeni nasekuphenduleni ukuze kufinyeleleke ekufinyeleleni okunyukile emikhiqizweni yezokwelapha esezingeni eliphezulu, ephephile, ephumelelayo nesezingeni eliphezulu. Umthelela: Imikhiqizo yezokwelapha esezingeni eliphansi nengamanga idala izinkinga eziningi kubo bobabili umuntu ophuza umuthi kanye nemindeni, umphakathi omkhulu, uhlelo lokunakekelwa kwezempilo kanye nochungechunge lokuhlinzeka. Uma imithi ingasebenzi ngendlela okufanele isebenze ngayo, ingahluleka ukwelapha noma ukuvimbela izifo, ibe nomthelela ekwandeni kwemithi elwa namagciwane, imoshe izinsiza eziyigugu futhi iholele ekulahlekelweni ukuzethemba emithini kanye nokungathembani abahlinzeki bezempilo.
translations.reference: No country remains untouched from this issue, and WHO has received reports of substandard or falsified medical medicines, vaccines and in vitro diagnostics from all regions of the world. Both generic and innovator medicines can be falsified, ranging from very expensive products for cancer to very inexpensive products for treatment of pain. WHO Member States have agreed on a comprehensive global strategy focused on prevention, detection and response to move towards achieving increased access to quality, safe, effective and quality medical products. Impact: Substandard and falsified medical products create many problems for both the individual taking the medicine and for families, the larger society, health care system and supply chain. When medicines do not work the way they should, they can fail to treat or prevent disease, contribute to antimicrobial resistance, waste precious resources and lead to a loss of confidence in medicines and distrust of healthcare providers.
translations.hypothesis: No country is immune to this issue, and the WHO has received reports of substandard or falsified medical products, devices, and diagnostics from all regions of the world. Both counterfeit and substandard products can be found, ranging from expensive cancer drugs to cheap painkillers. WHO member states have agreed on a global strategy focused on prevention, detection, and response to ensure increased access to safe, effective, and quality medical products. Impact: Substandard or falsified medical products pose significant risks to both patients and families, as well as to public health systems and the entire supply chain. When medicines do not work as intended, they can fail to treat or prevent diseases, contribute to the development of antimicrobial resistance, waste valuable resources, and erode public trust in medicines and healthcare providers.

translations.source: Lokhu kungaba nomthelela omubi kwezenhlalo-mnotho, kube nomthelela ekulahlekeni okuhlobene nokukhiqiza kanye nokwengeza izindleko ohlelweni lwezempilo lomuntu ngamunye kanye nolwezwe lonke. Kungenzeka kakhulu ukuthi bafinyelele iziguli ezimeni lapho kunokuvinjelwa ukufinyelela emikhiqizweni yezokwelapha esezingeni eliphezulu nephephile, ukubusa okungekuhle kanye namandla obuchwepheshe abuthakathaka. Nakuba le nkinga ithinta wonke amazwe, amazwe lapho kunezinhlelo zokulawula ezibuthaka noma ezingekho khona anomthwalo omkhulu wemikhiqizo yezokwelapha esezingeni eliphansi nengamanga. Emhlabeni owandayo womhlaba wonke, alikho izwe elinezinsiza ezanele namandla okubhekana nalolu daba lilodwa. Ngezinhlelo nezinqubo zomhlaba wonke ezifana neWHO Global Surveillance and Monitoring System kanye neWHO Member State Mechanism,
translations.reference: This can have a negative socioeconomic impact, contributing to related loss in productivity and adding expenses to the individual and national health system. They are most likely to reach patients in situations where there is constrained access to quality and safe medical products, poor governance and weak technical capacity. Although this issue impacts all countries, countries where there are weak or non-existent regulatory systems bear the greatest burden of substandard and falsified medical products. In an increasingly globalized world, no one country has sufficient resources and capacity to deal with this issue in isolation. Through global systems and processes such as the WHO Global Surveillance and Monitoring System and WHO Member State Mechanism,
translations.hypothesis: This can have negative socioeconomic consequences, leading to increased morbidity and mortality, as well as higher costs for individuals and the healthcare system as a whole. It is likely that patients will be denied access to high-quality, safe medications, leading to poorer health outcomes and a lack of confidence in the healthcare system. While this problem affects all countries, those with weak or non-existent regulatory systems are particularly vulnerable to the proliferation of substandard and falsified medicines. In an increasingly interconnected world, no country is immune to this threat. Global initiatives and mechanisms, such as the WHO Global Surveillance and Monitoring System and the WHO Member State Mechanism, are essential for addressing this issue.

translations.source: Amazwe Angamalungu asebenzisana ukuze avimbele, ahlonze futhi aphendule emikhiqizweni yezokwelapha esezingeni eliphansi nengamanga.
translations.reference: Member States are working together to prevent, detect and respond to substandard and falsified medical products.
translations.hypothesis: Member states are working together to prevent, detect, and respond to substandard and falsified medical products.

translations.source: Ukuvimbela ukuzibulala Uhlolojikelele: Bangaphezu kuka-700 000 abantu abafa ngokuzibulala minyaka yonke. Ngokuzibulala ngakunye, kulinganiselwa ukuthi kunemizamo yokuzibulala engaphezu kuka-20. Ukuzibulala kungenzeka kunoma yisiphi isigaba sempilo nakuzo zonke izifunda zomhlaba. Ngo-2019, ukuzibulala bekuyimbangela yesine yokufa kwabantu abaneminyaka engu-15-29 emhlabeni jikelele, kanti u-77% wabo bonke abantu abazibulalayo kwenzeka emazweni anemali engenayo ephansi naphakathi.
translations.reference: Suicide prevention Overview: More than 700 000 people die by suicide every year. For each suicide, there are an estimated more than 20 suicide attempts. Suicide can occur at any stage of life and in all regions of the world. In 2019, suicide was the fourth leading cause of death among 15–29-year-olds globally, and 77% of all suicides happened in low- and middle-income countries.
translations.hypothesis: Preventing Suicide: Globally, over 700,000 people die by suicide each year. For every suicide, it is estimated that there are more than 20 suicide attempts. Suicide can occur at any stage of life and in all regions of the world. In 2019, suicide was the fourth leading cause of death for people aged 15-29 globally, and 77% of all suicides occurred in low- and middle-income countries.

translations.source: Cishe u-20% wabantu abazibulalayo emhlabeni wonke ngenxa yokuzifaka ubuthi ngesibulala-zinambuzane. Nakuba ukuxhumana phakathi kokuzibulala kanye nezimo zempilo yengqondo (ikakhulukazi, ukudangala nokuphazamiseka kokusetshenziswa kotshwala) kanye nomzamo wangaphambilini wokuzibulala kusungulwe kahle emazweni anemali engenayo ephezulu, ukuzibulala okuningi kwenzeka ngokuxhamazela ezikhathini zobunzima. Ezinye izici eziyingozi zokuzibulala zihlanganisa isipiliyoni sokulahlekelwa, isizungu, ukubandlululwa, ukuhlukana kobudlelwane, izinkinga zezimali, ubuhlungu obungapheli nokugula, udlame, ukuhlukumeza, nezingxabano noma ezinye izimo eziphuthumayo zobuntu. Ukucwaswa okuhambisana nezimo zempilo yengqondo nokuzibulala kusho ukuthi abantu abaningi abacabanga ngokuzibulala abalufuni usizo. Ukuzibulala nemizamo yokuzibulala inomthelela omubi othinta imindeni, abangani, ozakwethu, imiphakathi kanye nemiphakathi.
translations.reference: Almost 20% of global suicides were due to pesticide self-poisoning. While the link between suicide and mental health conditions (in particular, depression and alcohol use disorders) and a previous suicide attempt is well established in high-income countries, many suicides happen impulsively in moments of crisis. Further risk factors for suicide include experience of loss, loneliness, discrimination, a relationship break-up, financial problems, chronic pain and illness, violence, abuse, and conflict or other humanitarian emergencies. Stigma around mental health conditions and suicide means many people thinking about suicide do not seek help. Suicides and suicide attempts have a ripple effect that impacts on families, friends, colleagues, communities and societies.
translations.hypothesis: Approximately 20% of suicides worldwide are linked to the use of pesticides. While the connection between suicide and mental health conditions (particularly depression and substance abuse disorders), as well as previous suicide attempts, has been well-established in wealthier countries, many suicides occur during periods of hardship. Other risk factors for suicide include the experience of loss, racism, discrimination, relationship breakdown, financial problems, chronic pain and illness, violence, abuse, and other stressful life events. The correlation between mental health conditions and suicide suggests that many people who contemplate suicide do not seek help. Suicide and suicide attempts have a devastating impact on families, friends, colleagues, communities, and society as a whole.

translations.source: Ukuzibulala: amaqiniso nezibalo emhlabeni jikelele Ukuzibulala emhlabeni wonke kubikwa ngo-2019 LIVE LIFE: umhlahlandlela wokuqalisa wokuvimbela ukuzibulala emazweni Ukuvimbela: Kuningi okungenziwa ezingeni lomuntu ngamunye, emphakathini kanye nakuzwelonke ukuvimbela ukuzibulala kanye nemizamo yokuzibulala. Indlela yeWHO's LIVE LIFE incoma ukungenelela okune okusebenzayo, okusekelwe ebufakazini okufanele kufakwe kuzo zonke izimpendulo zezwe zokuzibulala:
translations.reference: Suicide: facts and figures globally Suicide worldwide in 2019 report LIVE LIFE: an implementation guide for suicide prevention in countries Prevention: Much can be done at individual, community and national levels to prevent suicide and suicide attempts. WHO’s LIVE LIFE approach recommends four effective, evidence-based key interventions which should be included in every national response to suicide:
translations.hypothesis: Suicide: Facts and Figures Worldwide. Suicide globally was reported as [insert statistic here] in 2019. LIVE LIFE: A global framework for suicide prevention. Prevention: Much can be done at the individual, community, and national levels to prevent suicide and attempted suicide. The WHO's LIVE LIFE framework recommends interventions that are effective, evidence-based, and should be included in all national suicide prevention strategies:

translations.source: nciphisa ukufinyelela ezindleleni zokuzibulala xhumana nabezindaba ngokubika ngokuzibulala thuthukisa amakhono empilo yezenhlalo nemizwa entsheni bona kusenesikhathi, ahlole, aphathe futhi alandelele noma ubani othintwa imikhuba yokuzibulala. Izinsika eziyisisekelo zalokhu kungenelela wukuhlaziya isimo, ukusebenzisana kwezinhlaka eziningi, ukuqwashisa, ukuhlonyiswa ngamakhono, ukuxhasa ngezimali, ukugadwa, ukuqapha kanye nokuhlola.
translations.reference: limit access to the means of suicide interact with the media for responsible reporting of suicide foster socio-emotional life skills in adolescents early identify, assess, manage and follow up anyone who is affected by suicidal behaviours. The foundational pillars of the interventions are situation analysis, multisectoral collaboration, awareness raising, capacity building, financing, surveillance, monitoring and evaluation.
translations.hypothesis: Reduce access to methods of suicide, engage with media to report on suicide responsibly, and improve mental health and social skills among young people so that they can intervene early, assess, manage, and follow up with anyone affected by suicidal behavior. The key components of this intervention include assessment, multi-sectoral collaboration, awareness-raising, skills training, financial support, monitoring, evaluation, and research.

translations.source: Imizamo yokuvimbela ukuzibulala idinga ukubambisana nokusebenzisana phakathi kwemikhakha eminingi, okuhlanganisa ezempilo, imfundo, abasebenzi, ezolimo, ibhizinisi, ubulungiswa, umthetho, ukuvikela, ezombusazwe kanye nabezindaba. Ukuvimbela ukuzibulala ngokuvamile akuxazululwa ngokwanele ngenxa yokucwaswa kanye nokuntula ukwazi ukuthi ukuzibulala kuyinkinga enkulu yezempilo yomphakathi. Imiphakathi nayo idlala indima ebalulekile ekuvimbeleni ukuzibulala. Ikhithi yamathuluzi yokuxoxisana nomphakathi weWHO iqondisa imiphakathi ekuhlonzeni izinto ezibalulekile futhi isebenzise imisebenzi ngokwezimo zendawo. Impendulo yeWHO: Ukuphuthuma kokuthatha isinyathelo ukuvimbela ukuzibulala kuye kwaqashelwa futhi kwabekwa eqhulwini emazingeni aphezulu.
translations.reference: Suicide prevention efforts require coordination and collaboration among multiple sectors, including health, education, labour, agriculture, business, justice, law, defence, politics and the media. Suicide prevention is often not adequately addressed because of stigma and lack of awareness that suicide is a major public health problem. Communities also play a crucial role in preventing suicide. The WHO community engagement toolkit guides communities in identifying priorities and implementing activities according to local contexts. WHO response: The urgency to act to prevent suicides has been recognized and prioritized at the highest levels.
translations.hypothesis: Efforts to prevent suicide require collaboration and coordination among various sectors, including health, education, social services, agriculture, business, justice, law enforcement, government, and the media. Suicide prevention is often inadequately addressed due to a lack of awareness and understanding of the fact that suicide is a major public health problem. Communities also play a vital role in preventing suicide. The WHO's community engagement toolkit guides communities in identifying key issues and implementing appropriate interventions based on local contexts. WHO's response: The urgency of taking action to prevent suicide has been recognized and prioritized at the highest levels.

translations.source: Ukwehliswa kwamazinga okuzibulala emazweni kuyinkomba ku-UN Sustainable Development Goals (okuwukuphela kwenkomba yezempilo yengqondo), Uhlelo Olujwayelekile Lomsebenzi LweWHO kanye NoHlelo Olubanzi Lwezenzo Zezempilo Yengqondo lweWHO luka-2013–2030. Umbiko wokuqala weWHO wokuzibulala emhlabeni wonke, Ukuvimbela ukuzibulala: imfuneko yomhlaba wonke, eyanyatheliswa ngo-2014, okuhloswe ngayo ukwandisa ukuqwashisa ngokubaluleka kwezempilo yomphakathi kwemizamo yokuzibulala nokuzibulala nokwenza ukuvimbela ukuzibulala kube yinto ehamba phambili yezempilo yomphakathi. Ohulumeni bayakhuthazwa ukuthi basebenzise iphakethe leWHO LIVE LIFE lokungenelela okubalulekile okusekelwe ebufakazini ukuze kuvinjelwe ukuzibulala, futhi bathuthukise noma baqinise amasu aphelele kazwelonke okuvimbela ukuzibulala ngendlela yezempilo yomphakathi ehlanganisa izigaba eziningi. Ubuholi bukazwelonke kanye nokuxhumana ekuvimbeleni ukuzibulala kusekelwa ngokwengeziwe ukushicilelwa kwamasu kazwelonke okuvimbela ukuzibulala: inqubekelaphambili, izibonelo nezinkomba. Ibona ukuthi idatha nezibalo ziyadingeka ekukhulumeni nasekuthathweni kwezinqumo, iWHO yashicilela Ukuzibulala emhlabeni wonke ngo-2019: Izilinganiso Zezempilo Zomhlaba.
translations.reference: The reduction of suicide rates in countries is an indicator in the UN Sustainable Development Goals (the only indicator for mental health), WHO’s General Programme of Work and WHO’s Comprehensive Mental Health Action Plan 2013–2030. The first WHO world suicide report, Preventing suicide: a global imperative, published in 2014, aimed to increase awareness of the public health significance of suicide and suicide attempts and make suicide prevention a public health priority. Governments are encouraged to implement WHO’s LIVE LIFE package of effective and evidence-based key interventions for suicide prevention, and to develop or strengthen comprehensive national suicide prevention strategies in a multisectoral public health approach. National leadership and coordination on suicide prevention are further supported by the publication of National suicide prevention strategies: progress, examples and indicators. Recognizing that data and statistics are necessary for advocacy and decision-making, WHO published Suicide worldwide in 2019: Global Health Estimates.
translations.hypothesis: The prevalence of suicide rates across countries is an indicator of progress towards the UN Sustainable Development Goals (specifically, the health and well-being goal), the WHO's General Programme of Work, and the WHO's Comprehensive Mental Health Action Plan 2013–2030. The WHO's first global report on suicide, "Preventing Suicide: A Global Imperative," published in 2014, aimed to raise awareness of the importance of public health in addressing suicide and to make suicide prevention a priority for public health. Governments are encouraged to use the WHO's LIVE LIFE intervention package, a set of evidence-based strategies to prevent suicide, and to develop or strengthen national suicide prevention strategies that incorporate a public health approach and address multiple levels. National leadership and coordination in suicide prevention are further supported by the publication of national suicide prevention strategies: progress, examples, and indicators. Recognizing the need for data and statistics in communication and decision-making, the WHO published "Suicide Globally: Levels and Trends" in 2019: Global Health Estimates.

translations.source: Umbono weWHO wumhlaba lapho ukufa ngokuzibulala kungaseyona imbangela ehamba phambili yokufa nalapho abantu abazama noma abacabanga ukuzibulala bezizwa bekhululekile ukufuna usizo olunikezwa ngokusemandleni nangenhlonipho kubo bonke.
translations.reference: WHO’s vision is a world where death by suicide is no longer a leading cause of death and where people who are attempting or thinking about suicide feel comfortable seeking help which is provided capably and respectfully to all.
translations.hypothesis: The WHO's vision is a world where suicide is no longer a leading cause of death, and where people who are struggling with suicidal thoughts or have attempted suicide feel comfortable seeking help that is accessible and provided with respect to everyone.

translations.source: Ukuthuthuka okungashabalali Uhlolojikelele: Intuthuko esimeme yigama elibanzi elichaza izinqubomgomo, amaphrojekthi kanye nokutshalwa kwezimali okuhlinzeka ngezinzuzo namuhla ngaphandle kokudela impilo yemvelo, yezenhlalo kanye neyomuntu siqu esikhathini esizayo. Lezi zinqubomgomo zivame ukuchazwa njengeziluhlaza ngenxa yokuthi zigxile ekunciphiseni umthelela wentuthuko endaweni ezungezile. Kodwa ke, izinzuzo zentuthuko esimeme nazo zizwakala engxenyeni ebanzi yezempilo kanye nenhlalakahle yabantu, okuhlanganisa ukunciphisa ukungcola- kanye nezifo ezihlobene nemvelo, imiphumela yezempilo ethuthukisiwe kanye nokuncipha kokucindezeleka. Njengoba izinsongo zokuguquguquka kwesimo sezulu zibaluleka ngokwandayo, amasu aphumelelayo okunciphisa, ukuthuthukiswa kwezempilo kanye nokongiwa kwezindleko kunikeza izinzuzo eziningi kubabambiqhaza abahlukahlukene.
translations.reference: Sustainable development Overview: Sustainable development is a broad term to describe policies, projects and investments that provide benefits today without sacrificing environmental, social and personal health in the future. These policies are often described as green because they focus on limiting the impact of development on the environment. However, the benefits of sustainable development are also felt across a wide cross section of human health and well-being, including reductions in pollution- and environment-related disease, improved health outcomes and decreased stress. As the threats of climate change become increasingly important, win-win strategies for mitigation, health improvement and cost savings offer a range of advantages for various stakeholders.
translations.hypothesis: Sustainable Development: Overview: Sustainable development is a broad term that describes policies, projects, and investments that provide benefits today without compromising the environmental, social, and economic well-being of future generations. These policies are often referred to as "green" because they focus on minimizing the environmental impact of development. However, the benefits of sustainable development also extend to a wider range of social and economic areas, including reduced pollution and environmentally related diseases, improved health outcomes, and reduced stress. As the challenges of climate change become increasingly pressing, effective strategies for mitigation, health promotion, and cost savings offer significant benefits to a wide range of stakeholders.

translations.source: Isibonelo, ukunakekelwa kwezempilo okuluhlaza kungakhiqiza izinzuzo zezempilo zesiguli kanye nabasebenzi kuyilapho futhi konga amandla, kunciphisa izingozi zesimo sezulu futhi kudala ukonga izindleko zesikhathi eside. IWorld Health Organisation izibophezele ekuphishekeleni intuthuko esimeme kuwo wonke umsebenzi wayo wokusiza ukuvikela abantu bakusasa ezingozini zezempilo ezikhulayo zanamuhla. Umthelela: Amasu amaningi entuthuko esimeme anganikeza izinzuzo ezibalulekile kwezempilo, isimo sezulu kanye nemvelo, futhi izinzuzo zingabonakala ngokushesha. Isibonelo, izinhloso eziningi zezempilo nentuthuko zingafinyelelwa ngesikhathi esisodwa ngokubhekana nokungcoliswa komoya, okuyimbangela yokufa okulinganiselwa ezigidini ezingu-7 ngaphambi kwesikhathi ngonyaka. Ukukhishwa okungcolisayo kuhlanganisa izinto ezingcolisa umoya zesikhashana esifushane (SLCPs)—ikakhulukazi ikhabhoni emnyama, i-ozone nemethane—eziyingozi kakhulu empilweni kodwa ezinokuphila okufushane nje komkhathi.
translations.reference: For example, greener health care operations can generate patient and worker health benefits while also saving energy, mitigating climate risks and creating long-term cost savings. The World Health Organization is committed to pursuing sustainable development in all its work to help protect the people of tomorrow from the health growing health risks of today. Impact: Many sustainable development strategies can offer significant wins for health, climate and the environment, and the benefits can be seen almost immediately. For example, many health and development goals can be achieved simultaneously by tackling air pollution, which is responsible for an estimated 7 million premature deaths annually. Polluting emissions include powerful short-lived climate pollutants (SLCPs)—particularly black carbon, ozone and methane—which are very harmful to health but have only brief atmospheric lifetimes.
translations.hypothesis: For example, green healthcare can provide health benefits to both patients and staff, while also saving energy, reducing environmental risks, and creating long-term cost savings. The World Health Organisation is committed to promoting sustainable development in all of its work to help protect future generations from the growing health risks of today.
